title: Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)
warning: Utilize UTICalc for infants < 24 months to estimate probability before invasive catheterization.

start.title: Age Stratification
start.prompt: Is the patient age < 24 months or >= 24 months?

infant_uticalc.title: UTICalc Risk Probability
infant_uticalc.prompt: Apply UTICalc risk factors (gender, race, temp duration, lack of other source). Is the calculated probability >= 1%?

verbal_child.title: Symptoms and POC UA
verbal_child.prompt: Evaluate child for dysuria, pollakiuria, abdominal/back pain, and obtain clean-catch UA. Is UA positive for leukocyte esterase or nitrites?

obtain_urine.title: Urine Specimen Collection
obtain_urine.prompt: Obtain urine specimen via catheterization (preferred for non-toilet-trained) or clean-catch. Perform POC UA. Is leukocyte esterase or nitrite positive?

empiric_abx.title: Empiric Antibiotics & Culture
empiric_abx.prompt: Send urine for culture and initiate empiric antibiotics (Cephalosporin: Cefdinir or Cephalexin; or IV Ceftriaxone if toxic/unable to tolerate PO).
actions: Send urine specimen for culture & sensitivity
Initiate Cefdinir 14 mg/kg/day PO or Cephalexin 50-100 mg/kg/day PO
If toxic/vomiting, administer Ceftriaxone 50 mg/kg IV/IM

observation.title: Clinical Observation & Reassurance
observation.prompt: UTI is unlikely. Manage symptoms, observe for clinical deterioration, and follow up other fever sources.
actions: Acetaminophen or Ibuprofen for pain/fever
Maintain hydration PO
Safe discharge with return precautions if fever persists

title: SSTI Cellulitis / Abscess
warning: Exclude necrotizing fasciitis immediately in patients with out-of-proportion pain, crepitus, or rapid progression.

start.title: Initial Presentation
start.prompt: Examine the area for fluctuance, induration, and systemic signs of toxemia (fever, tachycardia, lethargy):

options.label: Systemic Toxemia or rapidly spreading erythema

localized_exam.title: Fluctuance & Abscess Determination
localized_exam.prompt: Is there a palpable fluid pocket (fluctuance) or concern for underlying abscess (bedside ultrasound useful)?

drainage_req.title: Incision & Drainage (I&D)
drainage_req.prompt: Perform local Incision & Drainage. Obtain wound culture. Prescribe oral antibiotics (TMP-SMX or Clindamycin for MRSA coverage) if cellulitis area is large or child is immunocompromised.
actions: Perform I&D under local anesthesia or procedural sedation
Pack loosely if cavity > 5cm
Obtain wound purulent fluid culture
Initiate TMP-SMX or Clindamycin PO

cellulitis_tx.title: Cellulitis Treatment
cellulitis_tx.prompt: Circle the margin of erythema. Initiate oral antibiotics (Cephalexin for MSSA/Strep; or Clindamycin/TMP-SMX if MRSA suspected). Schedule 24-48h clinical recheck.
actions: Mark erythema border with skin pen
Initiate Cephalexin 50-75 mg/kg/day PO or Clindamycin 30-40 mg/kg/day PO
Counsel family on returning if redness expands past the line

systemic_infection.title: Inpatient Admission & IV Antibiotics
systemic_infection.prompt: Admit patient for IV Clindamycin or Vancomycin. Consult Pediatric Surgery if necrotizing fasciitis is suspected.
actions: Obtain blood culture and CBC
Initiate IV Clindamycin 40 mg/kg/day or IV Vancomycin 15 mg/kg/dose
Urgent surgical consultation for suspected necrotizing infection
cantMiss: Necrotizing Fasciitis (Gas on X-ray, severe pain)

title: Gastroenteritis & Dehydration
warning: Assess for surgical causes (appendicitis, intussusception, malrotation) in patients with bilious emesis, severe localized abdominal pain, or blood in stool.

start.title: Severity Assessment
start.prompt: Evaluate patient for clinical signs of dehydration (skin turgor, mucous membranes, fontanelle, tear production, capillary refill):

options.label: Severe Dehydration / Shock (Cap refill >3s, cool skin, lethargic)
Mild to Moderate Dehydration (Dry lips, decreased tears, alert)

ort_trial.title: Oral Rehydration Therapy (ORT)
ort_trial.prompt: Administer Ondansetron (Zofran) if vomiting, and initiate ORT (5 ml every 2-5 mins of Oral Electrolyte Solution). Did the patient tolerate ORT?

options.label: Failed ORT (persistent vomiting, refuses PO fluids)

iv_rehydration.title: IV Rehydration (Mild/Moderate)
iv_rehydration.prompt: Place peripheral IV. Administer 20 mL/kg Isotonic Crystalloid (Normal Saline or Lactated Ringer's) over 1 hour. Re-evaluate. Consider adding 5% Dextrose once emesis ceases.
actions: Peripheral IV access
Crystalloid bolus 20 mL/kg IV over 1 hour
Recheck electrolytes (Sodium, Potassium) if IV fluid therapy is prolonged

iv_resusc.title: Emergent Fluid Resuscitation
iv_resusc.prompt: Secure IV/IO immediately. Administer 20 mL/kg Isotonic Crystalloid bolus over 10-20 minutes. Repeat as needed up to 60 mL/kg. Check blood gas, glucose, and electrolytes.
actions: Emergent IV/IO access
Isotonic Crystalloid 20 mL/kg bolus IV/IO over 15 minutes
Draw point-of-care glucose, blood gas, basic metabolic panel
Admit to inpatient / ICU

safe_discharge.title: Home Management Plan
safe_discharge.prompt: Discharge home with education on slow oral fluid administration, normal wet diapers, and strict return precautions.
actions: Discharge home, counsel on oral electrolyte maintenance therapy
Review signs of worsening dehydration (no urine for >8h, extreme lethargy)

title: Foreign Body Ingestion
warning: Button batteries and multiple magnets are surgical emergencies that can cause rapid necrosis and perforation.

start.title: Object Classification
start.prompt: Determine the type of swallowed object based on history/X-ray:

button_battery.title: Button Battery Location
button_battery.prompt: Obtain urgent biplanar X-ray (neck/chest/abdomen). Where is the battery located?

emergent_extraction.title: Emergent Endoscopy / Surgical Consultation
emergent_extraction.prompt: Keep NPO. Consult GI or Pediatric Surgery immediately for emergency endoscopic removal (within 2 hours for esophageal button batteries). Do not delay.
actions: Keep strictly NPO
Call GI / Surgery fellow on-call for emergency endoscopy
If button battery ingested <12 hours ago, administer honey (10 mL PO) to child > 1 year every 10 mins as a buffer

blunt_object.title: Blunt Object Location
blunt_object.prompt: Obtain X-ray. Where is the coin/object located?

battery_past_pylorus.title: Home Observation & Stool Checks
battery_past_pylorus.prompt: Discharge home. Advise regular diet. Confirm passage in stool within 7-10 days. Repeat X-ray in 4-7 days if passage is not confirmed.
actions: Discharge home; instruct parents to screen all stools
Repeat X-ray in 7 days to confirm clearance if not seen in stool

discharge_home.title: Clinical Observation at Home
discharge_home.prompt: Object past esophagus in asymptomatic patient. Discharge home. High probability of spontaneous passage. Return for fever, vomiting, abdominal pain, or blood in stool.
actions: Reassure family; normal diet and activity
Instruct parents on return symptoms (fever, pain, vomiting)

title: Throat Infection / Suspected PTA
warning: Airway protection is paramount. If stridor, drooling, or neck hyperextension (epiglottitis/retrotonsillar spread) is present, prepare for emergent airway.

start.title: Airway and Severity Triage
start.prompt: Assess for toxic appearance, drooling, or inability to open mouth (trismus):

options.label: Tonsillar swelling / Sore throat, stable airway

evaluate_throat.title: Abscess vs. Pharyngitis
evaluate_throat.prompt: Examine the tonsils. Is there asymmetric tonsillar swelling, uvular deviation, or localized fluctuance?

pharyngitis_workup.title: Pharyngitis / Strep Management
pharyngitis_workup.prompt: Obtain Rapid Strep test / Throat Culture. If positive, treat with Penicillin or Amoxicillin PO. Administer single-dose Dexamethasone PO for symptom relief.
actions: Perform Rapid Antigen Detection Test (RADT) for Strep
If RADT positive, prescribe Amoxicillin 50 mg/kg/day PO (Max 1000 mg) for 10 days
Consider Dexamethasone 0.6 mg/kg PO (Max 10 mg) x1 dose

pta_workup.title: Peritonsillar Abscess Management
pta_workup.prompt: Consider intraoral ultrasound or neck CT with contrast. Consult ENT for needle aspiration or incision and drainage. Administer IV Clindamycin or Ampicillin-Sulbactam + IV Dexamethasone.
actions: Perform intraoral ultrasound to confirm fluctuance
ENT consultation for drainage (needle aspiration/I&D)
Administer IV Ampicillin-Sulbactam 50 mg/kg/dose or IV Clindamycin 13 mg/kg/dose
Provide IV Dexamethasone 0.6 mg/kg x1

airway_urgency.title: Emergent Airway & ENT Mobilization
airway_urgency.prompt: Keep patient upright and calm. Do not examine throat if epiglottitis is suspected. Mobilize ENT and Anesthesia immediately. Prepare for emergency intubation/tracheostomy.
actions: Keep patient in position of comfort, minimize agitation
Consult ENT & Anesthesiology immediately for controlled airway placement
Administer IV Dexamethasone, prepare advanced airway cart

title: Suspected Physical Abuse
warning: Healthcare providers are mandated reporters. Immediate safety of the child and siblings is the primary ED goal.

start.title: Triage & Risk Factors
start.prompt: Evaluate for high-risk indicators: bruise on a non-mobile infant (<6 months), TEN-4-FACES bruising rules, injury mechanism mismatch, delayed presentation, or multiple fractures of different ages:

options.label: Bruise in infant < 6 months or highly suspicious fracture/injury
Minor injury with clear, compatible mechanism

abuse_protocol.title: Child Protection Protocol
abuse_protocol.prompt: Admit patient for safety. Perform complete skeletal survey (X-ray series). If age < 1 year, perform head CT to screen for abusive head trauma. Report immediately to CPS and Child Protection Team.
actions: File immediate report with Child Protective Services (CPS) / DSS
Order full skeletal survey (for children < 2 years with suspected abuse)
Order non-contrast Head CT (if < 1 year old or neurological symptoms)
Consult social work / Child Protection Team
Hold/Admit patient for safety; do not discharge until CPS safety plan is established
cantMiss: Abusive Head Trauma (Shaken Baby Syndrome / Subdural Hemorrhage)

standard_trauma_care.title: Standard Pediatric Trauma Management
standard_trauma_care.prompt: Treat injuries according to orthopedic or trauma guidelines. Safe to discharge if no suspicion of abuse.
actions: Standard splinting, wound closure, or pain management
Discharge home with standard outpatient follow-up
